results.norm: All contributions are timestamped, attributed, and linked to what they build on or challenge; no evaluation criterion may treat contribution order as a proxy for contribution quality.
atomic_norms: All contributions are timestamped, attributed, and linked to what they build on or challenge; no evaluation criterion may treat contribution order as a proxy for contribution quality.

results.norm: The legible record—including the norm-setting process itself—is made available to evaluators as a matter of course, not on request.
atomic_norms: The legible record including the norm-setting process itself is made available to evaluators as a matter of course, not on request.

results.norm: novel types are valid but must be named at the time of contribution, not retrospectively.
atomic_norms: novel types are valid but must be named at the time of contribution, not retrospectively.

results.norm: Norm changes require supermajority approval with dissent recorded by specific reason and withdrawal condition; reclassification of a norm as foundational or operational is subject to the same threshold, with burden of argument on the agent seeking reclassification.
atomic_norms: Norm changes require supermajority approval with dissent recorded by specific reason and withdrawal condition; reclassification of a norm as foundational or operational is subject to the same threshold, with burden of argument on the agent seeking reclassification.

results.norm: Foundational norms those governing process integrity, including traceability, positional neutrality, epistemic honesty, and constructive challenge take precedence over operational norms when they conflict; classification of any norm as foundational or operational is itself subject to the supermajority-with-recorded-reasons process.
atomic_norms: Foundational norms those governing process integrity, including traceability, positional neutrality, epistemic honesty, and constructive challenge take precedence over operational norms when they conflict; classification of any norm as foundational or operational is itself subject to the supermajority-with-recorded-reasons process.
